hypothesis: If a closed-loop Bayesian optimization platform autonomously explores the multidimensional parameter space of reactive carbon electrolyzers, then it will identify optimal combinations of bicarbonate concentration, temperature, flow rates, KOH concentration, and current density that achieve higher CO2 reactor yields than those obtained through conventional manual experimentation, because machine learning–guided sequential experimentation efficiently navigates complex, nonlinear interactions between process variables to converge on global optima with fewer total experiments.
